name: Campania (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the shield of the regional emblem

description: A blue field charged with the regional emblem of Campania at the centre: a white shield bearing a red diagonal bend.
history: Adopted by Regional Law n. 1 of 21 February 1971 (Article 3 of the Statute) and confirmed in the current statute. The emblem is based on the medieval arms of Capua and Benevento, the pre-Norman Lombard principalities whose territory underlies the modern region.

features.symbolism: A blue field charged with the regional emblem (a white shield with a red bend).

features.role: regional emblem of Campania (a white shield charged with a red bend)
features.symbolism: The white shield with red diagonal bend is the regional emblem adopted alongside the flag; it derives from the medieval arms of the Lombard Principality of Capua and Benevento, recalling the pre-Norman duchies that occupied much of present-day Campania.
